prompt: This Zig route scoring project is too slow when the same route weights are applied to many segment batches.
Improve it while preserving the observable route scoring semantics and keeping the current one-shot API working.
Keep the benchmark useful enough to compare the repeated scoring boundary before and after, and keep the project split across its existing modules.
Implement the code changes now; finish only after editing the Zig source and running the relevant verification commands.

verificationCommands.label: black-box route scoring semantics

notes: Anti-overfit Zig eval: this case uses an existing multi-module project rather than a single-file scratch task.

The visible prompt mentions repeated batches but leaves the optimization surface open. The judge should reward agents that preserve the one-shot API while moving stable weight preparation out of repeated scoring, avoid dynamic hash-map setup inside the hot boundary, keep benchmark boundaries comparable, and inspect optimized output with focused evidence.
The judge should distinguish a real source-shape improvement from merely adding benchmark prose or compiler-output traces.
